text.title: Load Balancer Health Checks Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Liveness and readiness MUST be served on different paths/endpoints; conflating them prevents safe rolling deploys.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Readiness MUST check critical dependencies (DB, cache, queue) with bounded timeout; liveness MUST NOT.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Health endpoints MUST return HTTP 200 plus a structured JSON body (`{status, deps:{...}}`); bare 200 with no body is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Probe timeout MUST be ≤500ms; longer probes mask failures and stall the LB loop.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: `fall` (consecutive failures to mark unhealthy) MUST be ≥ `rise` (consecutive successes to restore) to dampen flapping.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when single instance with no LB OR bare TCP check is sufficient and accepted.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
